_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Sunday March 15 | 5:49 a.m 

Bonjour Tremblant!

Winter is keeping the good times rolling! With 6cm of fresh snow in the last 24 hours and a total of 21cm total this week, the mountain is back in great shape! Temperatures will be warming up gradually throughout the day, perfect for enjoying every run to the fullest. Do keep in mind that moderate winds are expected to intensify toward the end of the afternoon. Please also note that following recent weather conditions, some areas on the mountain may have icy surfaces. To fully enjoy your experience safely, we invite you to stay on groomed runs and exercise extra caution.

Regarding alpine touring, while touring trails remain closed, ascension is still possible via Beauchemin, Algonquin, or Nansen. Our crews continue working hard to get all trails back in top shape, and we appreciate your understanding in the meantime. Finally, to make the most of those extra golden hours of daylight, our Soleil, Duncan, and Télécabine lifts will once again be running until 4:30 p.m. today, so no need to cut your day short!

Ontario March Break festivities continue on Place St-Bernard, come join the celebration!

First Tracks
  • Access to designated lifts, on Soleil, North and South side, as early as 7:45 a.m.
  • Ski on designated North Side slopes at 8 a.m.
Have a great one out there, enjoy every turn!

Mountain safety tip of the week: Remove your backpack.
In chairlifts, to ensure you are seated properly, remove your backpack and place it on your lap or, if space allows, on the seat. This reduces the risk of accidents or of straps getting caught in the lift.

Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
_inspectorSnowReport.Alert:
Due to recent weather conditions, several locations on the mountain have icy surfaces.

To fully enjoy your experience safely, we invite you to stay on groomed runs and avoid ungroomed areas.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReport: Happy Sunday! We received 6 cm of fresh snow in the last 24 hours, bringing this week's total to 21 cm. Groomed runs are in good shape, but icy surfaces have been reported in some areas — you are encouraged to stay on groomed runs and exercise extra caution. Touring trails remain closed, though ascension is possible via Beauchemin, Algonquin or Nansen. Soleil, Duncan and Télécabine lifts are extended until 4:30 p.m.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReportFrench: Bon dimanche! Nous avons reçu 6 cm de neige fraîche dans les dernières 24 heures, portant le total de la semaine à 21 cm. Les pistes damées sont en bonne condition, mais des surfaces glacées ont été signalées à certains endroits; nous vous encourageons à rester sur les pistes damées. La randonnée alpine demeure possible via Beauchemin, Algonquin ou Nansen. Les remontées Soleil, Duncan et Télécabine sont prolongées jusqu'à 16h30.
